remember: Students will be able to recall the plot and characters of the story 'The Enemy' by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle.
Students will be able to identify the main themes and ideas presented in the story.
Students will be able to list and explain the key events and turning points in the story.
understand: Students will be able to explain the symbolism and imagery used in the story to convey the themes.
Students will be able to analyze the character of Captain James Berowne and his relationships with others.
Students will be able to understand the historical context in which the story was written and its significance.
apply: Students will be able to apply the themes and ideas from the story to real-life situations.
Students will be able to use the literary devices used in the story to create their own writing.
Students will be able to analyze and interpret the characters and events in the story in different ways.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the ways in which the story explores the themes of identity, loyalty, and sacrifice.
Students will be able to evaluate the character of Captain James Berowne and his role in the story.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the story with other works of literature that explore similar themes.

ncert_objectives: To understand the plot, characters, and themes of the story 'The Enemy'.
To analyze the symbolism and imagery used in the story to convey the themes.
To evaluate the character of Captain James Berowne and his relationships with others.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and explain the main themes and ideas presented in the story.
To analyze and interpret the characters and events in the story in different ways.
To apply the themes and ideas from the story to real-life situations.
